title: Pultenaea recurvifolia
intro: Pultenaea recurvifolia, commonly known as the curved pea-flower bush, is a shrub species native to eastern Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in treating wounds and skin conditions such as acne. Bioactive constituents of Pultenaea recurvifolia include flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, as well as tinctures and extracts, which are utilized in traditional medicine for their purported health benefits.
taxonomy: Pultenaea recurvifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. This plant is native to Australia, and its classification places it within the subfamily Faboideae. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Pultenaea recurvifolia is often referred to as a legume due to its ability to fix nitrogen in the soil. The genus Pultenaea is part of the larger family, which includes over 19,000 known species. Pultenaea recurvifolia is further classified as a shrub or small tree, depending on the growing conditions.
